---
title: "RoleSim"
execute: 
  eval: false
  echo: true
format: 
   html:
      code-line-numbers: true
---

`RoleSim` (Jin et al. 2014) is a generalization of SimRank that computes similarity using maximum matching between neighbor sets, making it sensitive to automorphic similarity.

```r
RoleSim <- function(w, beta = 0.5) {
  do <- rowSums(w)
  di <- colSums(w)
  n <- nrow(w)
  z <- matrix(1, n, n)
  rownames(z) <- rownames(w)
  colnames(z) <- colnames(w)
  onei <- apply(w, 1, function(x) which(x == 1), simplify = FALSE)
  inei <- apply(w, 2, function(x) which(x == 1), simplify = FALSE)
  delta <- 1
  while (delta > 1e-10) {
    z.o <- z
    for (i in 1:n) {
      for (j in i:n) {
        if (i == j) next
        min.di <- min(di[i], di[j])
        max.di <- max(di[i], di[j])
        min.do <- min(do[i], do[j])
        max.do <- max(do[i], do[j])
        if (min.di != 0 & min.do != 0) {
          k <- z.o[inei[[i]], inei[[j]], drop = FALSE]
          d <- as.numeric(which(c(di[i], di[j]) == min.di)[1])
          mu.i <- sum(apply(k, d, max))
          k <- z.o[onei[[i]], onei[[j]], drop = FALSE]
          d <- as.numeric(which(c(do[i], do[j]) == min.do)[1])
          mu.o <- sum(apply(k, d, max))
          z[i, j] <- (beta * (mu.o + mu.i) / (max.do + max.di)) + (1 - beta)
        }
        if (min.di != 0 & min.do == 0) {
          k <- z.o[inei[[i]], inei[[j]], drop = FALSE]
          d <- as.numeric(which(c(di[i], di[j]) == min.di)[1])
          mu.i <- sum(apply(k, d, max))
          z[i, j] <- (beta * (mu.i / max.di)) + (1 - beta)
        }
        if (min.di == 0 & min.do != 0) {
          k <- z.o[onei[[i]], onei[[j]], drop = FALSE]
          d <- as.numeric(which(c(do[i], do[j]) == min.do)[1])
          mu.o <- sum(apply(k, d, max))
          z[i, j] <- (beta * (mu.o / max.do)) + (1 - beta)
        }
      }
    }
    z[lower.tri(z)] <- t(z)[lower.tri(z)]
    delta <- sum(abs(abs(z) - abs(z.o)))
  }
  return(z)
}
```
